items.title: Prompty: Arbitrary code execution via JavaScript frontmatter in TypeScript loader
items.description:
## Summary
The TypeScript Prompty loader used `gray-matter` without overriding executable frontmatter engines. `gray-matter` supports JavaScript frontmatter blocks such as `---js` and evaluates them while parsing. An attacker-controlled `.prompty` file could therefore execute arbitrary JavaScript during prompt loading.

## Affected package
- npm `@prompty/core` v2 prerelease line: `>= 2.0.0-alpha.1 < 2.0.0-beta.3`
- Fixed in `@prompty/core@2.0.0-beta.3`

The legacy v1 JavaScript runtime had a historical hardening change for this issue. During the v2 TypeScript runtime rebuild, the loader again called `gray-matter` directly and the vulnerable behavior was present in the v2 prerelease packages until `2.0.0-beta.3`.

## Impact
Applications that load untrusted `.prompty` files, user-provided prompt paths, or prompt bundles from less-trusted locations could execute arbitrary JavaScript in the host Node.js process during frontmatter parsing.

## Remediation
Upgrade `@prompty/core` to `2.0.0-

items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — From a Single Alert to 1000 Files Inside an Exposed WebDAV Malware Delivery Lab
id: 71551e09-37eb-556f-86c2-60f444dc230d
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to From a Single Alert to 1,000 Files: Inside an Exposed WebDAV Malware Delivery Lab (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: CRITICAL | Class: GENERIC
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/From a Single Alert to 1,000 Files: Inside an Exposed WebDAV Malware Delivery Lab
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/20
modified: 2026/07/20
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: critical
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — From a Single Alert to 1,000 Files: Inside an Exposed WebDAV Malware Delivery Lab — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-20T14:09:54Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "From a Single Alert to 1,000 Files: Inside an Exposed WebDAV Malware Delivery Lab", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX From a Single Alert to 1,000 Files: Inside an Exposed WebDAV Malware Delivery Lab Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:6611537; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-20, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.confidence_rationale: Intelligence confirmed: Item is listed in CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities; EPSS score 58.970 (very high exploitation probability); Source 'rapid7' is high-trust (tier-1 vendor research)

confidence_factors.signal: Item is listed in CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities

items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — Critical ServiceNow code execution flaw now exploited in attacks
id: b2fd05bd-4c25-5771-9eaa-d5177fe6116c
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-6875 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: HIGH | Class: RCE
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-6875
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/20
modified: 2026/07/20
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: generic
detection:
    selection_process:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'cmd.exe /c'
            - 'powershell -enc'
            - '/bin/sh -c'
            - 'wget http'
            - 'curl http'
    selection_webshell:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '/bash'
            - '/sh'
        ParentImage|contains:
            - 'w3wp.exe'
            - 'httpd'
            - 'nginx'
            - 'apache2'
            - 'tomcat'
    condition: selection_process or selection_webshell
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative scripts executing via web server
    - Deployment automation tools
level: high
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-6875 Remote Code Execution Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-20T14:09:54Z
// Deploy to: Microsoft Sentinel / Defender XDR
let lookback = 1d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe", "httpd.exe", "nginx.exe", "tomcat.exe", "java.exe")
    and FileName in~ ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "bash", "sh", "python.exe", "python3"))
    or (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("wget http", "curl http", "certutil -urlcache", "bitsadmin /transfer"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| where not(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("update", "install", "backup", "deploy"))  // filter known-good
| extend Severity = "HIGH", CVE = "CVE-2026-6875", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by Timestamp desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-6875 Exploitation Attempt - RCE"; flow:established,to_server; content:"/bin/sh"; nocase; content:"cmd.exe"; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:8678941; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-20, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.confidence_rationale: High confidence based on: CVSS score 9.5 (Critical); EPSS score 0.510 (very high exploitation probability)

confidence_factors.signal: CVE corroborated by 2 distinct sources: rss_cybersecuritynews_com_feed_, rss_www_bleepingcomputer_com_feed_

items.recommended_sla_action: URGENT PATCH — Critical severity, deploy fix within 72 hours

items.title: Public PoC released for Critical ServiceNow Sandbox RCE Vulnerability
items.description:
ServiceNow has released security updates for a critical vulnerability in its AI platform after researchers published a proof of concept demonstrating pre-authentication remote code execution. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-6875, is a sandbox escape issue that could allow an unauthenticated attacker to run code within a vulnerable ServiceNow instance.     - Deployment automation tools
level: high
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-16227 Remote Code Execution Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-20T14:09:54Z
// Deploy to: Microsoft Sentinel / Defender XDR
let lookback = 1d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe", "httpd.exe", "nginx.exe", "tomcat.exe", "java.exe")
    and FileName in~ ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "bash", "sh", "python.exe", "python3"))
    or (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("wget http", "curl http", "certutil -urlcache", "bitsadmin /transfer"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| where not(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("update", "install", "backup", "deploy"))  // filter known-good
| extend Severity = "HIGH", CVE = "CVE-2026-16227", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by Timestamp desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-16227 Exploitation Attempt - RCE"; flow:established,to_server; content:"/bin/sh"; nocase; content:"cmd.exe"; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:5304390; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-20, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)

items.title: Gitea has insufficient permission checks for Composer package source links
items.description:
### CVE Description
Gitea versions up to and including 1.26.1 have insufficient permission checks for Composer package source links, which can expose private or internal package source information.

### Summary
A critical vulnerability has been discovered in Gitea. It was already reported via (security@gitea.io) from (dev@noscope.com), and submitted an encrypted report.
items.recommended_sla_action: SCHEDULED PATCH — Apply in next maintenance window

items.title: CVE-2026-16225: A security flaw has been discovered in davenardella snap7 up to 1.4.3. The impacted element is the function TSnap7Peer::
items.description: A security flaw has been discovered in davenardella snap7 up to 1.4.3. The impacted element is the function TSnap7Peer::NegotiatePDULength of the file src/core/s7_peer.cpp. The manipulation of the argument PDULength results in out-of-bounds write. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks.
items.apex_ai_summary: [MEDIUM] CVE advisory. Risk score 6.3/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.

apex.ai_summary: [P2] CVE / Vulnerability - moderate severity (Risk 6.3/10). No MITRE ATT&CK techniques mapped - limited adversarial context. 1 indicator detected - upgrade to Pro for full IOC access. AI confidence: LOW (22%) - limited source data available.
apex.recommended_action: HIGH PRIORITY - Patch within 72 hours. Monitor affected assets. Review threat hunting queries for related TTPs.

items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-16225 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-20T14:09:54Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-16225", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
